content:
Hello everyone,

I'm developing a driver under the Rust for Linux framework and need to use semaphores for synchronization. I noticed there's no semaphore abstraction currently implemented under rust/kernel/sync. What confuses me is that there's a rust_semaphore.rs example in samples/rust, but no one has implemented a semaphore abstraction yet.

Is there a need to add a semaphore abstraction in rfl? Has anyone already started working on this?
There is an old thread about it https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/YIdUf1C5VtWn%2FD2G@localhost.localdomain/
But all of that is pretty old and probably outdated. You should take a look at the rust branch on the RfL github, it should be in there but will need updating
I don't know of anybody working on updating it (doesn't mean somebody isn't of course), so that job is probably up for grabs if you are interested in turning it into something that could be merged
But note that things generally don't get merged without an in-tree usecase. So, do you have one of those? :)
(obviously that is not required to develop something or get it reviewed, that's just the barrier to actually merging)

Thanks! I haven't found a specific in-tree use case yet, but I'll check out the existing work. I might also try using primitives like mutex and condition variables to achieve the synchronization I need.
